revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.
== List of Taisha ==
{| class="wikitable" !Shrine name !Former shrine name ! [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Imperial Rank]] ![[Engishiki]] ! Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]] |- |[[Kumano Taisha]] |Kumano Jinja | Kokuhei Taisha |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] | [[:en:Matsue|Matsue City]], Shimane Prefecture |- | colspan="5" |[[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] |Suwa Jinja | Kanpei Taisha |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] | Suwa City, [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] |- |[[:en:Keta Taisha|Keta Taisha]] |Keta |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] | Tarui [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Town]], Fuwa District, Gifu Prefecture |- |[[:en:Tado Taisha|Tado Taisha]] |Tado Jinja | Kokuhei Taisha |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Inukami District, Shiga Prefecture |- |[[:en:Takebe taisha|Takebe taisha]] |Takebe Jinja | Kanpei Taisha |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] | [[:en:Ōtsu, Shiga|Otsu City]], Shiga Prefecture |- Kanpei Chusha |[[Kokushi genzaisha]] | Nachikatsuura Town, Higashimuro District, Wakayama Prefecture |- |[[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] |Munakata Jinja | Kanpei Taisha |[[:en:Myōjin [[:en:Kurume|Kurume City]], Fukuoka Prefecture |}
== Former Taisha == There are some shrines which used to call themselves Taisha but later changed their names {| class="wikitable" !Shrine name ! [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Imperial Rank]] ![[Engishiki]] !Notes |- |[[:en:Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)|Hikawa Shrine]] |[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei Taisha]] |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] |The word "Hikawa Taisha" appears on the bag of votive offerings |- |[[:en:Hirano Shrine|Hirano Shrine]] |[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei Taisha]] |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] |There used to be a sign that said "Hirano Taisha" on the gate |- |[[:en:Hiraoka Shrine|Hiraoka Shrine]] |[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei Taisha]] |[[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]] |A sign that reads "Hiraoka Taisha" is still present in front of the old station of Hiraoka |- |[[:en:Ikoma Shrine|Ikoma Shrine]] | [[:en:Fuken-sha|Fuken-sha]] |[[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] | |}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|[[:en:Kōjien|Kōjien]]|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 [[:en:Shikinai Taisha|Shikinai Taisha]] listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the [[:en:Empire of Japan|Empire of Japan]]. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|WD=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien}}|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha}} listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan}}. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11438310]] Wikidata name: taisha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|WD=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien}}|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha}} listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan}}. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]]
{{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|WD=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien}}|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha}} listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan}}. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

{{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|WD=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien|1=Kōjien}}|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha|1=Shikinai Taisha}} listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan|1=Empire of Japan}}. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Taisha (shrine)]][[ja:大社]][[ko:다이샤]][[simple:Taisha (shrine)]][[tr:Taisha]]
{{Short description|Type of Shinto Shrine}}
'''Taisha''' is a term used to refer to a Rank of {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s.
A {{nihongo|''taisha''|大社}}(the characters are also read ''ōyashiro'') is literally a "great shrine"<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> that was classified as such under the old system of shrine Ranking, the {{nihongo|[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|shakaku]]|社格}}, abolished in 1946.<ref name="IK">Iwanami {{nihongo|{{ill|Kōjien|en|Kōjien|WD=Q1192611|lt=Kōjien|1=Kōjien}}|広辞苑}} Japanese dictionary</ref><ref name=":2">{{cite web |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |title=Myōjin taisha |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120213073711/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=260 |access-date=2023-04-10|archive-date=2012-02-13 }}</ref>
== History == Originally Taisha referred to the 203 [[:en:Myōjin Taisha|Myōjin Taisha]]<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web |last=Inoue |first=Nobutaka |title=Myōjin taisha |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=[[Kokugakuin University]] Encyclopedia of Shinto |language=en}}</ref> or the 492 {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|WD=Q134917288|lt=Shikinai Taisha|1=Shikinai Taisha}} listed in the [[Engishiki]]
The term became more strict in the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|WD=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan|1=Empire of Japan}}. In that time it almost always referred to [[Izumo-taisha]]. After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.

After the war, many shrines with the Rank of taisha, such as the former Kanpei Taisha and the former Kokuhei Taisha Shrine, started calling themselves taisha.
[[Izumo-taisha|Izumo Taisha]] wants to be the only shrine to use the name.
